plan.title: chore(security): pin GitHub Actions to SHAs + least-privilege permissions + PyPI OIDC (parent #1069)

narratives.Description: chore(security): pin GitHub Actions to SHAs + least-privilege permissions + PyPI OIDC (parent #1069)
narratives.Overview:
## Parent

#1069

## What to build

GitHub Actions supply-chain hardening — two parts that ship in the same PR because they touch the same workflow files:

1. **Pin every `uses:` to a commit SHA.** Every `uses:` line in `.github/workflows/**` resolved to a floating ref (`@master`, `@main`, `@vN`) is a supply-chain entry point: an upstream compromise executes in CI with the job's full environment. Replace floating refs with resolved commit SHAs and add a `# vX.Y.Z` comment naming the tag so Dependabot (landed in Slice 1, #1070) can still propose bumps.

2. **Least-privilege `permissions:` blocks.** Build / publish jobs MUST declare a top-level (or job-level) `permissions:` block scoped to exactly the privileges they need. Default `GITHUB_TOKEN` scope is too broad for any job that touches secrets.

3. **Migrate PyPI publish to OIDC trusted publishing.** Stop storing a long-lived `PYPI_API_TOKEN` in `secrets`; configure PyPI's trusted-publisher feature so the publish job obtains a short-lived token via OIDC. Requires a one-time human registration on `pypi.org` → project → Publishing → "Add a new pending publisher" with the workflow filename, environment, and ref pattern.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every `uses:` in `.github/workflows/**` is pinned to a 40-character commit SHA (no `@master` / `@main` / floating-major refs on security-sensitive actions)
- [ ] Each pin has a `# vX.Y.Z` comment naming the upstream tag for human readability and Dependabot tracking
- [ ] Every build/publish job declares a `permissions:` block (top-level or job-level) with the minimum required scopes; the default broad-scope token is no longer in use for jobs that see release secrets
- [ ] The PyPI publish workflow uses `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish` with OIDC (no `password:` / `PYPI_API_TOKEN` input); the PyPI project has a registered trusted publisher matching this repo's workflow + ref
- [ ] A dry-run or test-PyPI publish succeeds end-to-end via the new OIDC path
- [ ] All new tests pass
- [ ] `task check` passes

## Type

HITL

The OIDC trusted-publishing migration requires a human to log into PyPI's web UI, navigate to project settings, and register this repo as a trusted publisher (workflow filename, environment, ref). No GitHub-side automation can do that step. The rest of the slice (SHA pinning, `permissions:` blocks) is mechanical and AFK; it's the PyPI-side enrollment that drags the whole slice into HITL.

## Blocked by

- Blocked by #1071 (resolve OSV advisories first so action bumps don't collide with pinning churn)

references.title: Issue #1072: chore(security): pin GitHub Actions to SHAs + least-privilege permissions + PyPI OIDC (parent #1069)
